title: Investment Accelerator Act of 2025
plain_summary: The Investment Accelerator Act of 2025 creates a new government office to help companies make very large investments of more than one billion dollars. This new office will be part of the Department of Commerce and will help investors understand and follow government rules more easily. The office will have a director who must tell Congress about their work every year. The goal is to make it faster and simpler for big companies to invest lots of money in the United States.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Investment Accelerator Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Definitions
sections.text: In this law, these terms mean the following. Appropriate congressional committees means the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives. Department means the Department of Commerce. Executive Director means the Executive Director of the Investment Accelerator who is appointed under section three part c. Investment Accelerator means the United States Investment Accelerator that is created under section three part a. Secretary means the Secretary of Commerce.

sections.title: Section 3  United States Investment Accelerator
sections.text: The government will create a new office. If Congress provides the money for it, the Secretary of Commerce will work with the Secretary of the Treasury and the Assistant to the President for Economic Policy to create an office in the Department of Commerce called the United States Investment Accelerator. The Investment Accelerator has seven main jobs. First, it will help speed up investments worth more than one billion dollars in the United States by helping investors get through government rules and processes more easily. Second, it will reduce government rules when the law allows it. Third, it will help investors access and use national resources of the United States when appropriate and when the law allows it. Fourth, it will help create research partnerships with national labs in the United States. Fifth, it will work with state governments in all fifty states to reduce barriers and increase both domestic and foreign investment in the United States. Sixth, it will coordinate and oversee the activities of the CHIPS Program Office in the Department of Commerce. Seventh, it will find existing ways, exceptions, and opportunities in federal law that can help foreign and domestic investors while protecting national security. There will be an Executive Director who leads the Investment Accelerator. The Secretary of Commerce will choose and appoint this person. The Executive Director will carry out all the jobs listed above for the Investment Accelerator. The Director can hire legal, business transaction, operational, and support staff to help with the work of the Investment Accelerator.

sections.title: Section 4  Reports
sections.text: The Executive Director must write a report about what the Investment Accelerator has done and send it to the appropriate congressional committees. The first report must be sent no later than one year after this law is passed. After that, the Executive Director must send a report every year.
